data.description:
## What

Stale present-tense bash-engine language survives in CI workflow comments and one
source docstring, describing machinery that was removed in the bash→Python migration
(`README.md:599-601` confirms the bash engine and its `.sh` suites are gone).

Genuinely-stale spots to fix:
- `.github/workflows/test.yml:8-9` — references a `test_bash_suites.py` collector
  running "standalone bash suites under tests/scripts/test-*.sh" (neither exists).
- `.github/workflows/external-integration.yml:45-46` — says the fixture initializes a
  store "via the bash engine, which shells out to jq + git".
- `src/rebar/_commands/leaf.py:9` — docstring says code "mirrors those functions
  byte-for-byte so the per-command bash suite passes against either implementation."

Do NOT touch the legitimate **past-tense** provenance notes (e.g. the many
`tests/scripts/**` "In-process port of tests/scripts/test-*.sh" docstrings,
`docs/bash-migration.md`, archive docs) — those are accurate history.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] The three stale spots no longer assert present-tense bash machinery.
- [ ] `grep -rn "test_bash_suites" .github src` returns no present-tense claim that it
      runs.
- [ ] Legitimate historical/past-tense port notes are left unchanged.

Files: `.github/workflows/test.yml` (lines ~8-9),
`.github/workflows/external-integration.yml` (lines ~45-46),
`src/rebar/_commands/leaf.py` (line ~9).
data.title: P1: Purge stale bash-engine language from CI comments & leaf.py docstring
